div1.entryFree: Adhere, to be in accordance: they do no more a. and keep place together than . . ., Wiv. II, 1, 62. every thing --s together, Tw. III, 4, 86. nor time nor place did then a. Mcb. I, 7, 52. With to: a shepherd's daughter, and what to her --s, Wint. IV, 1, 28 (what is in accordance with her condition). two men there are not living to whom he more --s, Hml. II, 2, 21 (to whom his mind is more congenial, who, as v. 12 expresses it, are 'more neighboured to his youth and haviour').
